title: How Model Context Protocol (MCP) actually works
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol that enables AI agents to discover and interact with external tools and data without custom integration code. Google Cloud explains how MCP differs from traditional APIs by supporting probabilistic AI reasoning and standardizing agent-to-resource connections through a client-server architecture with tools, prompts, resources, and context components.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video explains how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) lets AI agents discover and interact with external tools and data without custom integration code. It contrasts MCP with traditional APIs, noting that MCP supports probabilistic AI reasoning rather than fixed, deterministic calls. The core takeaway is the client-server architecture that standardizes agent-to-resource connections, organized around tools, prompts, resources, and context components.
